heroText: Abuja is a capital city in Nigeria, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Abuja
highlights.text: Abuja is a capital city in Nigeria, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.label: 1970s
history.text: The location was eventually designated in the centre of the country in the early 1970s as it signified neutrality and national unity.
6 on 4 February 1976, which initiated the removal of the Federal Capital from Lagos to Abuja.
The International Planning Associates (IPA) was commissioned in June 1977, by the Federal Capital Development Authority (FCDA) to produce the Abuja Master Plan and its regional grid.
The final report was submitted to the pioneer Executive Secretary of FCDA, Alhaji Abubakar Koko, on 15 February 1979.

history.label: 1980s
history.text: Abuja is a planned city built mainly in the 1980s based on a master plan by International Planning Associates (IPA), a consortium of three American planning and architecture firms made up of Wallace, Roberts.

history.label: Today
history.text: The Gwandara speaking people in Federal Capital Territory are mostly found in AMAC and Bwari Area Council.

history.label: Civic role
history.text: Some of their villages/settlements are Wuse, Asokoro now relocated to Sabon Wuse, Aso in Niger and other neighbouring states.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: In light of the ethnic and religious divisions of Nigeria, plans had been devised since Nigeria's independence to have its capital in a place deemed neutral to all major ethnic parties, and also near.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The move of Nigeria's Capital to Abuja was controversial, and the biggest opposition to it was led by Obafemi Awolowo.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Without the unstinting efforts of these experts, the momentum now exhibited at the beginning of the actual implementation of the new capital could not have been achieved.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The plan itself represents a milestone in the process of building the new capital city.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: More detailed design of the central areas of the capital, particularly its monumental core and the Central Business District, was accomplished by Japanese architect Kenzo Tange with his team of city planners at Kenzo.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Most countries relocated their embassies to Abuja, and many maintain their former embassies as consulates in Lagos, the commercial capital of Nigeria.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Squatter settlements and towns have spread rapidly in and outside the city limits.
